# OSLogStore

**Class**

A set of entries from the unified logging system.

## Declaration

```swift
class OSLogStore
```

## Overview

Instances of this class represent a fixed range of entries and may be backed by a `logarchive` or your Mac’s local store.

In Swift, Use the [doc://com.apple.oslog/documentation/OSLog/OSLogStore/getEntries(with:at:matching:)] function to retrieve a filtered array of log entries.

In Objective-C, use instances of this class to create [doc://com.apple.oslog/documentation/OSLog/OSLogEnumerator] objects. One store can support multiple `OSLogEnumerator` instances concurrently.

## Creating Log Stores

- **init(url:)**: Creates a log store based on a log archive.
- **local()**: Creates a log store representing the Mac’s local store.

## Accessing Position

- **position(date:)**: Returns a position representing the time specified.
- **position(timeIntervalSinceEnd:)**: Returns a position representing time since the end of the time range that the entries span.
- **position(timeIntervalSinceLatestBoot:)**: Returns a position representing time since the last boot in the series of entries.

## Accessing Entries

- **getEntries(with:at:matching:)**: Returns a sequence of log entries filtered by the parameters passed in.

## Initializers

- **init()**
- **init(scope:)**

## Enumerations

- **OSLogStore.Scope**

## Read Log Entries

- **OSLogEnumerator**: An enumerator that can access and list log entries.

## Inherits From

- NSObject

## Conforms To

- CVarArg
- CustomDebugStringConvertible
- CustomStringConvertible
- Equatable
- Hashable
- NSObjectProtocol
